revision_summary: Four targeted fixes required before production: (1) SPOUSE PROTOCOL SCOPE: Cherry_pick_moments[2] currently surfaces the spouse exclusion failure at 11:16 only. Add a second cherry_pick entry at 01:29 ('My husband, Andy. K. Gotcha.') flagging this as the earlier protocol failure point — the schema protocols.spouse_decision requires proactive inclusion when a co-title holder is identified, not reactive recovery when exclusion is attempted. (2) OUTCOME TAG SCHEMA: trajectory_verdict.outcome_dispute proposes 'booked / conditionally qualified / fragile' as a replacement tag. Rephrase to map to schema outcomes schema: 'appointment_booked=true; appointment_held risk elevated due to two structural vulnerabilities: co-title holder Andy excluded (protocols.spouse_decision violation) and appointment conditioned on rep presence (21:13).' Remove the invented compound tag. (3) MISSED MOMENTS: Add cherry_pick entries for (a) 14:21 — Ashley's consent statement and Meg's weak auto-dialer apology response as a missed anchor opportunity (analysis_directives.trust_building_micro_trades); (b) 12:22 — second flip opportunity on 'what are the benefits cost' (analysis_directives.flip_opportunity_detection); (c) 08:01 — W2/in-house explanation as scope creep instance (coaching_philosophy.setter_scope_discipline). (4) SCHEMA STATUS MARKERS: Cherry_pick_moments[0] coaching_implication references 'action-first contact-complaint protocol' as if it is an established schema standard. Add a note that protocols.excessive_contact_complaint is explicitly TBD in the schema and this call is a candidate exemplar for Kenny's next calibration session — not a validated floor standard. Similarly, remove or qualify 'shape: energy_built' in trajectory_verdict as Stage 3-generated descriptive language, not a schema schema value.
